rationale: The citing editorial explicitly states it “extends Huber, Kamakura, and Mela’s (2014) historical review” by covering 2013–2019 and mapping topic/method/domain intersections, indicating the 2014 paper is a direct baseline rather than a passing mention. The matched DOI (10.1509/jmr.51.1.02) corresponds to the cited paper “A Topical History of JMR,” which aligns closely with the citing paper’s core research question about JMR’s topical evolution.
